assertions.predicate: Throughput rises with headcount, peaks mid-run, then declines as coordination overhead dominates: the final value is at most 85% of the steps-20-100 peak.
assertions.rationale: The scenario's red-teamed core shape: more hands, then plateau, then decline (Brooks channels + band penalties outgrow added capacity).

assertions.predicate: By the end of the run the org spends at least 40% of its capacity on coordination.
assertions.rationale: At 25 people flat + meeting-heavy, channel load per person (12 channels each) must visibly dominate capacity.

assertions.predicate: Cohesion degrades below 65 after the ~15-person band is crossed.
assertions.rationale: The red-teamed expectation: cohesion starts degrading as the 15-member inner-circle threshold is crossed.

assertions.predicate: Entropy rises by at least 18 points over the run.
assertions.rationale: Scaling 12 to 25 with zero structural change must register as a large, visible entropy shift.

assertions.predicate: Even the luckiest 10% of Monte Carlo runs end above 40 entropy - the degradation is structural, not noise.
assertions.rationale: Band-level check that the collapse is robust across seeds, not a mean-path artifact.

assertions.predicate: A routine decision under a 3-layer stack takes 6-9 working days (settled mean over the last 10 steps).
assertions.rationale: SI Labs pattern: 2-3 days per layer x 3 layers = 6-9 working days for a decision that should take hours.

assertions.predicate: Three layers make decisions at least 2.2x slower than one layer in the same org.
assertions.rationale: The tax is relative: same org, same work, only the ownership stack differs.

assertions.predicate: The 3-layer org carries at least 1.5x the work-in-progress of the 1-layer org - items stack up waiting for approval.
assertions.rationale: WIP accumulation is the visible symptom of approval queues (red-team stress test 3).

assertions.predicate: Higher-layer overrides keep occurring at a visible rate: at least 2 more override events accumulate between steps 40 and 59.
assertions.rationale: Override events with partial rework are the mechanism that makes layers cost throughput, not just time. The cumulative series is the robust carrier: per-step medians of sparse integer event counts are degenerate (p50 = 0).

assertions.predicate: The single-layer baseline produces zero overrides across the whole run.
assertions.rationale: Controls that overrides are a layering effect in the model, not background noise.

assertions.predicate: Even the slowest 10% of runs keep settled routine latency under 16 working days - the model does not explode.
assertions.rationale: Tail sanity: queueing variance widens but does not diverge at 0.92 top-layer utilization.

assertions.predicate: In the SH=3 org, entropy RISES by at least 5 points after AI injection (step 15 to end) - AI on broken structure makes things worse.
assertions.rationale: The product's central claim: layering AI on low Structural Health amplifies dysfunction (red-team stress test 4).

assertions.predicate: In the otherwise-identical SH=7 org, the run WITH AI settles at least 1.5 entropy points BELOW the same org without AI - injection helps when structure is healthy.
assertions.rationale: The contrast that makes the story honest: AI is not poison - structure decides the sign of its effect. Counterfactual form is robust to the mean-path mode's discrete event spikes at run end.

assertions.predicate: Total brittleness events in the SH=3 org run at least 1.3x the SH=7 org's total by the end.
assertions.rationale: Direct check of the red-teamed amplification claim at the scenario endpoints (cumulative form: per-step medians of sparse counts are degenerate).

assertions.predicate: From step 35 onward, the SH=3 entropy band (p10) sits strictly above the SH=7 band (p90) at the default seed - the before/after visual is separable, not two overlapping fuzzy bands.
assertions.rationale: The launch-centerpiece chart must be visually unambiguous - the two bands must be golden-asserted to stay separated rather than merely hoped to.

assertions.predicate: Right after injection (steps 16-22) SH=3 throughput runs at least 5% above its pre-injection level (steps 8-14), within a 3% tolerance: the dysfunction is FASTER - that is the seduction.
assertions.rationale: Red-team stress test 4: throughput increases while entropy worsens; both must be visible or the story reads as 'AI bad'. Mechanism: the M11 approval-bandwidth multiplier - AI routes work into the broken structure faster.

assertions.predicate: By the settled window, the SH=3 org WITH AI carries at least 5 more entropy points than the same org that did nothing.
assertions.rationale: The punchline in counterfactual form: on broken structure, injecting AI is worse than not acting.

assertions.predicate: Per-pod cohesion stays healthy (>= 65) all the way to 8 pods - each pod is fine.
assertions.rationale: The 'healthy teams' half of the healthy-teams-sick-org insight (red-team stress test 5).

assertions.predicate: Org-level entropy rises by at least 15 points as pods multiply.
assertions.rationale: The 'sick org' half: inter-pod channels and unscaled decision structure dominate.

assertions.predicate: The gap between team cohesion and org health widens by at least 12 points - the two lines visibly diverge on one chart.
assertions.rationale: Design red-team fix: the Multi-Level Health divergence is the shareable insight and must be structural.

assertions.predicate: Inter-pod channels grow at least 6x while pod count grows 2.67x - superlinear coordination surface.
assertions.rationale: Quadratic pair growth plus rising misalignment is the cliff mechanism; 6x >> 2.67x proves superlinearity.

assertions.predicate: Even in the luckiest 10% of runs the final team-vs-org health gap exceeds 8 points.
assertions.rationale: The divergence must survive Monte Carlo noise to be chart-worthy.

assertions.predicate: The AI prioritization agent routes routine work at most 0.6x the human PM's latency - the acceleration is real.
assertions.rationale: Red-team stress test 2: initial acceleration on routine prioritization is what makes the Hollow Middle tempting.

assertions.predicate: Across the early window (steps 8-14) the hollow team's cumulative throughput averages at least 8% above the human-PM team's - work accelerates initially.
assertions.rationale: The seductive first fortnight, before the edge cases arrive. Re-anchored from the single step t=10 - where the ratio of small-integer cumulative medians jumps discretely (1.000 -> 1.14 with nothing between) - to the mean of the pointwise ratio over [8,14], a continuous carrier that no longer straddles a knife-edge under the Monte-Carlo p50 semantics.

assertions.predicate: Total brittleness events in the hollow team run at least 2x the human-PM team by the end.
assertions.rationale: Novel decisions hitting an AI router without human judgment is the failure mechanism (red-team stress test 2). Zero-denominator convention (§11.1): the human-PM team's novel failures (humanNovelFailureBase) are sparse enough that its cumulative total can be 0, giving ratio = +infinity, which satisfies ratioAbove; the paired hmBrittlenessFloorMc pins the hollow numerator to >= 1.5 so the ratio is never the indeterminate 0/0.

assertions.predicate: Stakeholder Communication coverage is at most 0.5 (red) in the hollow team - the function the AI cannot actually do.
assertions.rationale: Red-team requirement: the function coverage map must show Stakeholder Communication under-covered when the human PM is removed.

assertions.predicate: Execution coverage stays at least 0.8 (green) - the hollow team's problem is not capacity.
assertions.rationale: Red-team requirement: Execution and routine prioritization stay green while judgment functions go red - that contrast is the insight.

assertions.predicate: The hollow team ends at least 8 cohesion points below the human-PM team.
assertions.rationale: Front-line teams lose the human relationship layer of their PM (red-team stress test 2; WEF/AMCIS cohesion findings).

assertions.predicate: The hollow team pays strictly less coordination tax (ratio <= 0.995) - AI routing has lower handoff friction.
assertions.rationale: Red-team stress test 2: coordination tax drops for routine routing - part of why the trap works.

assertions.predicate: The median Monte Carlo run sees at least 1.5 brittleness events over 60 steps - the failures are not a tail scare-story.
assertions.rationale: Median-run realism for the brittleness narrative.

assertions.predicate: With review capacity unbounded (default null), review wait sits at the review dwell (1 working day) at every step - the M20 capacity gate is inert and reproduces v1 review byte-for-byte.
assertions.rationale: Neutral-identity lock for M20: reviewCapacityPerStep = null must leave the realized review sojourn at reviewDwellDays, proving the queue changes nothing at default (the calibration-safety contract). reviewWaitDays is deterministic here (every sojourn = reviewDwellDays = 1), so within [1,1] at tol 0 holds under the p50 semantics. Exact identity, not a provisional bound.

assertions.predicate: With an AI agent in the middle approval seat, settled first-pass decision latency runs at most 0.9x the all-human stack's - the routing acceleration is real.
assertions.rationale: P6 configurator acceptance, half 1: decisionLatency here is the all-class org first-pass latency, and routine work - the ~60% majority of the mix (taskMixRoutineOrg) - is the dominant driver of the aggregate drop; an aiAgent layer's low layerLatencyFactor and higher layerCapacityFactor (§9.9) speed that routine majority through the stack.

assertions.predicate: The AI-middle stack accumulates at least 2 novel-task brittleness events over the run - the acceleration comes bundled with novel exposure.
assertions.rationale: P6 configurator acceptance, half 2: an aiAgent seat's layerNovelExposure (§9.9) routes novel work into the M9 brittleness path even with no org-wide injection.

assertions.predicate: The all-human stack produces zero brittleness events - the novel exposure is entirely attributable to the AI seat, not the scenario.
assertions.rationale: The clean counterfactual that makes lcNovelBrittleRises meaningful: swap only the middle seat's type and the brittleness appears.

assertions.predicate: The median Monte Carlo run of the AI-middle stack sees at least 1 brittleness event - the novel exposure is not a mean-path artifact.
assertions.rationale: Band-level realism: the per-layer novel exposure survives Monte Carlo, not just the deterministic accumulator.

assertions.predicate: Three co-equal owners per seat make settled first-pass decisions at least 1.4x slower than a single owner in the same org - the consultation/relitigation surcharge is real.
assertions.rationale: M19 latency surcharge: each added co-equal owner adds consultation rounds before a decision clears (muLatencySurchargeRate; ~1.7x at mu=3). decisionLatency samples first-pass approvals (overrideCount == 0; §11.1), so the ratio isolates the per-seat surcharge; the diffusion-driven queue growth in coOwned only widens it. Direction is well supported (Darley & Latané 1968: interventions both fewer AND slower with more responsible parties). (Calibration-confirmed against the engine golden harness, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.90 — the bound 1.4 is kept predicate-consistent and cleared by a wide margin, with the 0.1 tolerance as the noise cushion.)

assertions.predicate: The three-co-owner stack accumulates at least 1.4x the overrides of the single-owner stack by the end - accountability multiplicity multiplies relitigation.
assertions.rationale: M19 feeds the M8 override probability as an additional multiplicative diffusionMean = 1 + overrideDiffusionGain·(mu−1) ≈ 1.8 at mu=3 (Latané 1981 Social Impact Theory, felt responsibility ∝ N^−0.5). Both runs are 3-layer so both override; the ratio isolates the diffusion multiplier. singleOwner is the base-model override rate (identical to prioritizationTax@threeLayer). Zero-denominator convention (§11.1) keeps the ratio valid if the single-owner cumulative is small. (Calibration-confirmed against the engine golden harness, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.48 (31/21 cumulative overrides); the bound 1.4 is kept predicate-consistent — the tightest of the org set, cleared to 1.26 effective by the 0.1 tolerance, which absorbs the ±1-event lumpiness of a ~21-31 count ratio.)

assertions.predicate: The three-co-owner org carries at least 1.2x the work-in-progress of the single-owner org - diffused ownership piles up relitigated, half-reset work.
assertions.rationale: Combined visible symptom: more overrides re-inject WIP at wipResetFraction (M8) and the latency surcharge holds tasks in-pipeline longer, so queued+inProgress+blocked rises. Mirrors ptWipRatio; the bound is lower than prioritizationTax's 1.5 because both runs share the same 3-layer stack and only the multiplicity differs. (Calibration-confirmed against the engine golden harness, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.82 — the bound 1.2 is kept predicate-consistent and cleared by a wide margin (52% over the raw bound).)

assertions.predicate: Even at the Monte Carlo median, the three-co-owner stack runs at least 1.3x the overrides of the single-owner stack - the diffusion effect is structural, not a mean-path artifact.
assertions.rationale: Band-level realism for the relitigation narrative (mirrors hmBrittlenessFloorMc / lcBrittleFloorMc). Using the p50 ratio makes the claim magnitude-independent and robust to the sparse-integer override counts; the zero-denominator convention (§11.1) satisfies ratioAbove if the single-owner median is 0. (Retuned against the engine golden harness MC p50, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.48; the 1.3 bound is held with margin for the lumpy count ratio.)

assertions.predicate: A diffuse committee (three co-equal owners) accumulates at least 1.15x the overrides of the SAME committee at a single accountable owner - the accountability-diffusion cost now appears on the committee seat, driven entirely by layerOwnerCount.
assertions.rationale: M19 additive diffusion: committeeDiffuse's layerOwnerCount 3 on the middle seat raises diffusionMean = mean over l in 2..=L of (1 + overrideDiffusionGain*(mu_l-1)) to ~1.4 (one of two averaged seats at mu=3), scaling the M8 override probability; committeeSingle (layerOwnerCount 1) is the v1 committee with diffusionMean = 1. Both runs share the identical committee §9.9 relay factors, so the ratio isolates the diffusion cost. (Calibration-confirmed against the engine golden harness, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.25 (25/20 cumulative overrides); the bound 1.15 is kept predicate-consistent — cleared to 1.035 effective by the 0.1 tolerance, absorbing the ±1-event lumpiness of the ~20-count ratio.)

assertions.predicate: A single-owner committee still accumulates FEWER overrides than the all-human stack (ratio at most ~0.95) - the many-eyes relay benefit persists, so the committee is not inverted into a penalty (anti-inversion evidence).
assertions.rationale: At mu = 1 on both runs the M8 override PROBABILITY o(t) differs only through the distortion term: the committee middle seat's layerDistortionFactorCommittee (0.5) vs humanPm (1.0) lowers layerDistortionMean, so the distortion-driven override component is strictly lower for the committee - the v1 many-eyes relay discount, untouched by the amendment. (The committee seat's slower layerLatencyFactorCommittee / layerCapacityFactorCommittee act on queueing, M6, a second-order influence on the override COUNT - which is precisely why this bound is provisional and the load-bearing claim is the direction, ratio < 1.) (Calibration-confirmed against the engine golden harness, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 0.83 (20/24 cumulative overrides); the bound 0.95 is kept predicate-consistent and cleared with a wide margin below 1 — the relay discount is real.)

assertions.predicate: Even at the Monte Carlo median, the diffuse committee runs at least 1.1x the overrides of the single-owner committee - the diffusion cost is structural, not a mean-path artifact.
assertions.rationale: Band-level realism for the diffusion-cost claim (mirrors adOverrideRatioMc). The p50 ratio is magnitude-independent and robust to sparse-integer override counts; the §11.1 zero-denominator convention satisfies ratioAbove if the single-owner median is 0. (Retuned against the engine golden harness MC p50, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.25; the 1.1 bound is held with margin for the small-count ratio.)

assertions.predicate: Two bosses with no tiebreaker make settled decisions at least 1.2x slower than a single boss - dual authority strangles the decision.
assertions.rationale: M19 at mu=2, tiebreaker=0 applies the shared muLatencySurchargeRate to the terminal matrix seat (diffusionLatencyFactor_L ≈ 1.35; there is NO separate dual-authority coefficient - the matrix folds entirely into M19). Davis & Lawrence 1977 'decision strangulation'; McKinsey 2016/2019: distributed accountability decides materially slower. The bound is conservative because only the terminal of two seats carries the surcharge, so the aggregate ratio sits between 1 and ~1.35. (Calibration-confirmed against the engine golden harness, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.24 — the bound 1.2 is kept predicate-consistent; it is the tightest raw margin of the matrix set but the 0.1 tolerance clears it to 1.08 effective, robust for a stable EMA-latency ratio.)

assertions.predicate: The no-tiebreaker matrix relitigates at least 1.3x as many decisions as the single-boss org - either boss can reopen what the other cleared.
assertions.rationale: M19 multiplies M8 override probability by diffusionMean ≈ 1.4 at mu=2 for the L=2 stack (Davis & Lawrence 'power struggles'; McKinsey 2022: multiple veto-holders create loops/reversals a single decider removes). singleBoss's 2-layer stack provides the base override channel the diffusion factor scales; §11.1 zero-denominator convention keeps the ratio valid. (Retuned against the engine golden harness, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.50 (15/10 cumulative overrides); the 1.3 bound is held with a robust margin for the lumpy small-count ratio.)

assertions.predicate: Naming a clear decider recovers single-boss decision speed: the tiebreaker=1 matrix runs no slower than the single-boss org (ratio at most ~1.0).
assertions.rationale: The tiebreaker control (Rogers & Blenko 2006 'Who Has the D?': one named decider unclogs the matrix bottleneck). In the unified M19 form diffusionFactor = 1 + overrideDiffusionGain·(mu−1)·(1 − tiebreaker) collapses to 1 at tiebreaker=1, the latency/drop channels to 0, and the diffusion multiplies existing probabilities/service draws (adds no RNG draw), so dualBossClearDecider is byte-identical to singleBoss and the ratio is EXACTLY 1.0 (an exact identity, not a provisional estimate). The 0.05 tolerance is slack only.

assertions.predicate: The two-bosses-with-a-decider matrix is at least ~15% faster than the deadlocked two-boss matrix - the tiebreaker is the documented fix, not the dual reporting itself.
assertions.rationale: The load-bearing tiebreaker contrast, robust to the exact surcharge coefficient: dualBossClearDecider recovers to single-boss latency while dualBossNoTiebreak carries the full mu=2 terminal-seat surcharge, so the ratio ≈ 1/1.35 ≈ 0.74. Isolates the [0,1] tiebreaker as the leverage point (McKinsey 2022 single point of accountability). (Retuned against the engine golden harness, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 0.81; the 0.85 bound is held — it asserts the documented ≥15% speed-up with margin above the measured value.)

assertions.predicate: Even at the Monte Carlo median, the no-tiebreaker matrix relitigates at least 1.2x the single-boss org - the dual-authority reversal loop survives noise.
assertions.rationale: Band-level realism for the relitigation claim; p50 ratio is magnitude-independent and the §11.1 zero-denominator convention satisfies ratioAbove if the single-boss median is 0. (Retuned against the engine golden harness MC p50, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 1.50; the 1.2 bound is held with a robust margin.)

assertions.predicate: With review capped below the AI completion rate, the review queue grows by at least 10 items over the run - the unstable rho ≥ 1 regime.
assertions.rationale: M20 capacity gate: the producers (five AI execution agents) deliver a ~1.46 completions/step stream into review — the all-AI team is itself execution-limited on the novel-task minority, so raw AI speed does not all reach review — outrunning the single human reviewer (reviewCapacityPerStep 1, one clearance/step), so the queue grows ~linearly at (completionRate − reviewCapacity) ≈ 0.46/step (Goldratt ToC: surplus upstream of the constraint accumulates as WIP). Window starts at step 10 to skip warm-up transients. (Calibration-confirmed against the rebuilt scoped team calibrator, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured queue rise ≈22 over [10,59]; the bound 10 is kept predicate-consistent and cleared with a wide margin.)

assertions.predicate: By the end of the run the capped team has shipped at most 0.85x the cumulative done-throughput of the uncapped team - done-throughput plateaus at the reviewer's rate no matter how fast the AI agents complete work.
assertions.rationale: The Theory-of-Constraints invariant (Goldratt 'The Goal'): system throughput-to-done is capped by the slowest stage. Bottleneck done-rate ≈ reviewCapacity (1/step) while the unbounded control clears the full ~1.46/step completion stream, so the capped team's cumulative done-throughput ends well below the uncapped control's. Raising producer speed upstream cannot lift the plateau; only elevating the constraint can. METRIC BASIS (mini-G2 approved 2026-07-04): the metric is the ratio of the cumulative `cumThroughput` series (the §7.2 running total of completions, already the carrier for hmEarlyThroughputBoost) at the final step, NOT the per-step `throughput` count. At the reviewer's ~1/step rate the per-step `throughput` is a small integer whose p50 cannot distinguish 1 from 1.46 (it reads 0.95, masking the plateau); the cumulative total is a large integer (bottleneck ≈58 vs unbounded ≈85 over 60 steps) whose p50 ratio robustly measures the plateau. Calibration-confirmed against the rebuilt scoped team calibrator, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured 0.6824, which the 0.85 ratioBelow bound (kept predicate-consistent) clears with ~20% margin. cumThroughput is an existing team output series (no new series / schema change); referencing it mirrors hmEarlyThroughputBoost.

assertions.predicate: By the end of the run, completed work waits at least 4 working days for review in the capped team - lead time diverges as the queue grows.
assertions.rationale: Little's Law (W = L / throughput): with a growing queue L cleared at the fixed reviewCapacity (1/step), review wait rises superlinearly (M/M/1 rho/(1−rho) as rho → 1). Anchored well below the tens-of-days the divergent queue implies, and far above the well-run few-hours latency (Sadowski et al. 2018) the unbounded control tracks. (Calibration-confirmed against the rebuilt scoped team calibrator, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured final review wait ≈19.3 working days; the bound 4 is kept predicate-consistent and cleared with a wide margin.)

assertions.predicate: With reviewCapacityPerStep null (default), the review queue stays bounded and small (at most ~5 items) all run - unbounded parallelism reproduces the v1 review behaviour, no ToC plateau.
assertions.rationale: The neutral-identity anchor for M20: null capacity means every dwell-elapsed completion clears each step, so the queue never accumulates a runaway backlog and done-throughput is not gated - the v1 behaviour. reviewQueueDepth holds only the in-dwell population (≈ completionRate × reviewDwellDays ≈ 1.46). (Calibration-confirmed against the rebuilt scoped team calibrator, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured unbounded final queue ≈1 (p50); the bound 5 is kept predicate-consistent, comfortably above the small in-dwell population.)

assertions.predicate: Even the luckiest 10% of Monte Carlo runs end with at least 10 items queued for review in the capped team - the bottleneck is structural, not a mean-path artifact.
assertions.rationale: Band-level check that the review queue builds across seeds (mirrors ccEntropyFloorMc). rho ≥ 1 makes the queue grow deterministically in expectation, so even the p10 path accumulates a large backlog over 60 steps. (Calibration-confirmed against the rebuilt scoped team calibrator, seed 42, 500 iters, 2026-07-04: measured p10 final queue ≈14; the bound 10 is kept predicate-consistent and cleared with margin.)
